fields.icd_conflict_detail: The referral form lists ICD-10 code E11.9 (Type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications), while both the clinical notes and prescription consistently document E11.65 (Type 2 diabetes mellitus with hyperglycemia); the more specific and clinically authoritative code E11.65 from the clinical notes and prescription was selected as the resolved code.

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: The clinical notes and prescription both consistently document E11.65 (Type 2 diabetes mellitus with hyperglycemia), which supersedes the less specific E11.9 on the referral form; confidence is high given two-to-one document agreement and the clinical notes representing the treating physician's direct assessment.

fields.priority_reason: This is a stable chronic condition (Type 2 diabetes with hyperglycemia) requiring a CGM for ongoing glucose monitoring; while an ICD conflict was present it was resolved with high confidence, and the equipment is important for metabolic management but not acutely life-threatening.
fields.notes: 1) Authorization Reference Number is missing from the referral form — follow-up with adjuster Kevin Osei at k.osei@amtrust.com or (216) 555-0189 required before processing. 2) The referring practice is South Bay Orthopedic Group, which is an orthopedic specialty; prescribing a CGM for diabetes management may warrant confirmation that the treating physician is coordinating with an endocrinologist or primary care provider. 3) Clinical notes mention 'post-operative assessment' and 'wound healing,' suggesting a surgical event may have occurred, but no surgical procedure is documented in any form — this may be a template artifact or an undisclosed procedure that should be clarified. 4) The medical justification provided ('return-to-baseline functional status' and 'reduce caregiver burden') is generic and may not satisfy payer-specific CGM medical necessity criteria — a more detailed justification tied to hyperglycemia management may be needed for authorization. 5) Referral was submitted 11/28/2026, six days after the injury date of 11/22/2026.

fired_rules.source: CMS DME MAC Jurisdiction D — Prior Authorization Requirements
fired_rules.message: Workers compensation claim — authorization reference number required for E0143 billing. Missing auth ref blocks claim submission.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

email_body:
Dear Kevin Osei,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding a pending durable medical equipment (DME) referral for your insured and am writing to request assistance in resolving an outstanding item needed to move forward.

**Patient:** Raymond Morris
**Claim Number:** WC-2026-084475

Upon reviewing the referral, we have identified the following required field that must be obtained before processing can continue:

- **Authorization Reference Number (auth_ref)** — This item is currently acting as a hard block and is preventing routing of the referral until it is resolved.

To avoid any delay in care for the patient, we kindly ask that you provide the above information **within 24 business hours** of receiving this email.

Please reply directly to this message with the requested information, or feel free to contact our office if you have any questions or require clarification.

We appreciate your prompt attention to this matter and look forward to your response.

Best regards,

**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME
